title: SEC-3: 32-bit length-decode truncation in skipValue and inline helper
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go:32-43` (skipValue case 2); `compiler/types/type.go:227-232` (`emitConsumeBytesLenAt`).

**What:** `byteLen` is decoded into `uint64` but immediately converted with `int(byteLen)` and checked only for `< 0`. On `GOARCH=386/arm/wasm` (32-bit `int`), values in `(MaxInt32, MaxInt64]` truncate to small positive ints, bypassing both the `< 0` check and the `postIndex > l` bound. CLAUDE.md "Common review caveats" lists this exact pitfall under "32-bit length safety".

**Fix:** Before `int(byteLen)`, add `if byteLen > uint64(math.MaxInt) { return io.ErrUnexpectedEOF }`. Apply in both `skipValue` and `emitConsumeBytesLenAt`. Add a test that runs under `GOARCH=386`.
close_reason: merged via #63 (commit 9e26682) — bead missed by earlier sweep

title: CR-4: Self-referencing message without optional emits infinite Go struct
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_struct.go:51` (no cycle detection); generator emits value-type message fields by default.

**What:** `message Tree { Tree child = 1; }` is valid proto3. wiresmith emits `Child Tree` (value type), which `go vet` and `go build` both reject as a recursively-sized struct. The pointer-option (`(wiresmith.options.pointer) = true`) and `optional` keyword both fix it, but the generator silently emits the broken struct rather than detecting the cycle.

**Fix:** Add a SCC-based cycle check during planning, following only non-optional/non-repeated/non-pointer-option/non-oneof/non-map edges. On detection, return an actionable error pointing at the offending field and suggesting `optional` or `(wiresmith.options.pointer) = true`.
close_reason: Already shipped in merged PRs #68, #72, #69, #67, #70 respectively

title: SEC-2: Pre-scan default: break only exits switch, not outer loop
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go:138-141`.

**What:** When `preTyp ∈ {3,4,6,7}` (legacy groups + reserved), the `default: break` only terminates the inner `switch`; `preIdx` is unchanged, so the next iteration treats payload bytes as a new tag. This inflates field counts and feeds SEC-1. The post-switch guard at line 141 only catches genuinely out-of-bounds `preIdx`.

**Fix:** Either label the outer `for` and `break OUTER`, or set `preIdx = l` in the default branch, or simply abort the pre-scan entirely on unknown wire type (the main loop is the source of truth — pre-scan is an optimisation). The "abort the optimisation" option is safest.
close_reason: Already shipped in merged PRs #68, #72, #69, #67, #70 respectively

title: CR-3: Empty .proto produces uncompilable Go
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/generator.go:271-309` (`generateFile`); pertinent imports added unconditionally at `emit_unmarshal.go:163-165`.

**What:** A `syntax = "proto3"; package empty;` with no messages or enums still emits a `.pb.go` that imports `protohelpers`/`protowire` but never uses them. CLAUDE.md flags this as a known caveat; nothing in the generator guards it.

**Fix:** In `generateFile`, short-circuit if `messages == 0 && enums == 0` — either skip the file entirely or emit only the package clause. Add a generator test with an empty fixture proto.
close_reason: Closed

title: CR-2: Negative-zero floats stripped on marshal (silent corruption)
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/types/fixed64.go:42-43` (`EmitSize`, `EmitMarshal` use `if access != 0`); `compiler/types/fixed32.go:43` mirrors this for `float`.

**What:** In Go, `-0.0 != 0` is `false`, so a singular `double`/`float` set to `-0.0` is treated as a default value and elided from the wire. On round-trip it reads back as `+0.0`. `math.Signbit` is lost. The official `google.golang.org/protobuf` library guards this with `if v == 0 && !math.Signbit(v) { return }` (see its `codec_gen.go`).

**Fix:** Emit `if math.Float64bits(access) != 0` (or, equivalently, `Float32bits`) so any non-zero bit pattern — including negative zero — serialises. Add a unit test in `test/basic/numeric_test.go` that marshals `math.Copysign(0, -1)` and asserts `math.Signbit` survives the round-trip.
close_reason: Fix: marshal/size now use math.FloatNNbits(v) != 0 so -0.0 survives the wire round-trip; matches google.golang.org/protobuf. Includes test coverage in test/basic/numeric_test.go.

title: SEC-1: Pre-scan slice pre-allocation is attacker-amplifiable
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go:144-156` (pre-scan size hint), `99-141` (pre-scan loop).

**What:** The pre-scan counts field-number occurrences then does `m.X = make([]Span, 0, count)`. A malicious 1 MB payload of tiny tag-for-field-1 + zero-length entries can produce ~500 000 occurrences, allocating ~125 MB of capacity for `[]Span` (struct size ≈ 250 B). Combined with SEC-2 below, the count itself becomes attacker-controlled.

**Fix:** Cap pre-allocated capacity by a sanity bound: `cap = min(count, (l-iNdEx)/minBytesPerElement)` where `minBytesPerElement` is the smallest legal wire encoding for the element type (e.g., `1` for varint scalar tag + minimum body). For message-typed repeated fields, the minimum is 2 bytes (tag + zero-length). Easy formula: `cap = min(count, l/2)`. Add a fuzz seed that exercises this exact shape.
close_reason: Already shipped in merged PRs #68, #72, #69, #67, #70 respectively

title: CR-1: Reset() panics on nil receiver
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_reset.go:15`

**What:** Emits `func (m *T) Reset() { *m = T{} }` with no nil guard, while the same generator emits `String()` *with* a nil guard at line 17–19. `CLAUDE.md` "Common review caveats" lists `Reset()` as one of the methods that must handle nil gracefully — this regressed silently because `String()` got the treatment and `Reset()` did not.

**Fix:** `func (m *T) Reset() { if m == nil { return }; *m = T{} }`. One branch on a cold path; trivially testable.
close_reason: merged via #64 (commit 28613ea); bead never got closed at the time

title: Remove mktemp -d dance from generate-ours conformance step
description:
## Intent

Delete the temp-dir workaround in `Makefile:120-123` once the CLI accepts
positional `.proto` file arguments. This ticket is the canary: when it
can be closed cleanly, the protoc-ergonomic-parity work has actually
landed in user-visible form.

## Why this exists

Today the Makefile does:

```make
$(eval CONF_TMP := $(shell mktemp -d))
@cp proto/conformance/test_messages_proto3.proto "$(CONF_TMP)/"
$(WIRESMITH) --proto_path="$(CONF_TMP)" --out=gen --module=$(MODULE)
@rm -rf "$(CONF_TMP)"
```

This exists purely because wiresmith walks all `.proto` files in
`--proto_path` and emits for every one. The conformance step wants only
`test_messages_proto3.proto`, so it isolates it.

Filing as a tracking ticket (not the implementation ticket) so the
workaround removal is explicit and the parity claim is auditable.

## What changes

Once the dependency ships:

```make
$(WIRESMITH) --proto_path=proto/conformance --out=gen --module=$(MODULE) \
  proto/conformance/test_messages_proto3.proto
```

…with no temp-dir, no cp, no cleanup.

## Acceptance criteria

- `Makefile:120-123` reduced to a single direct invocation.
- `make generate-ours` produces an identical `gen/` tree.
- `make conformance` still passes (same 696 passing / 5 expected fails).

## Source

Identified during 2026-05-26 review of wiresmith CLI vs. protoc/vtproto/
gogoproto. The mktemp dance was the most concrete ergonomic regression
spotted in the review.
notes: Already done — Makefile conformance step now reads: $(WIRESMITH) --proto_path=proto/conformance --out=gen --module=$(MODULE) proto/conformance/test_messages_proto3.proto (Makefile:120). Landed with PR #76 (wiresmith-7eh's positional-args feature). 'make generate-ours' produces identical gen/ output verified during that PR.
close_reason: delivered alongside wiresmith-7eh in PR #76 — the tracking ticket's acceptance criteria are all met

title: Switch to per-source-file Go output (one .pb.go per .proto)
description:
## Intent

Emit exactly one `.pb.go` per input `.proto`, named by extension swap
(`foo.proto` → `foo.pb.go`), matching protoc-gen-go's per-file emission
model. Today wiresmith aggregates all `.proto` files in a proto package
into a single Go file.

## Why this exists

The source-relative output path migration in wiresmith-ahz defines the
output as `<--out>/<source-file's-path-relative-to---proto_path>.pb.go`.
That contract is impossible to honor while the generator emits per
*package*: two `.proto` files in the same package (e.g., `common.proto`
and `types.proto` both declaring `package example.v1;`) currently produce
one shared output file, not `common.pb.go` + `types.pb.go`.

Splitting this out of wiresmith-ahz so the emission-model change is
explicit and reviewable on its own, rather than buried inside path
derivation.

## What changes

- `compiler/generator/generator.go`: the per-proto-package aggregation
  in `generateFile` (and its callers) becomes per-input-file. Cross-file
  imports within the same package must resolve via Go imports
  (same-package files in Go can reference each other's identifiers
  without imports, so this is mostly an organizational change in how
  `*FileGenerator` is keyed and how registration init blocks are
  partitioned).
- Generated registration init blocks: each `.pb.go` registers only its
  own types; the aggregate-per-package model goes away.

## Acceptance criteria

- For an input tree with `pkg/common.proto` and `pkg/types.proto` in the
  same proto package, output is `pkg/common.pb.go` + `pkg/types.pb.go`,
  not a single file.
- `TestGenerateMatchesCheckedIn` updated to reflect the new per-file
  layout.
- Cross-file type references within the same package still compile.

## Relation to other tickets

This is a precondition for wiresmith-ahz (source-relative paths). The
path-derivation work is meaningless if all files in a package still
collapse to one output.

## Source

Identified during 2026-05-26 review of wiresmith CLI vs. protoc/vtproto/
gogoproto.
notes:
Per-source-file emission is already implemented — verified by inspecting generator.go (generateFile operates per fd, outputPathFor uses filepath.Base(fd.Path())) and by running a probe: two .proto files declaring the same proto package produce two distinct .pb.go + two _reflect.pb.go files, each with its own init() and file_*_rawDesc. Cross-file refs in the same Go package resolve via Go's same-package namespace and compile cleanly. Added TestGeneratePerSourceFileOutput as a regression pin so this contract can't silently regress and break the wiresmith-ahz source-relative path migration.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/75
close_reason: Already implemented; added regression test pinning per-source-file emission (acceptance-criteria check).

title: Accept .proto file paths as positional arguments to scope generation
description:
## Intent

Match protoc's positional-argument convention: `wiresmith [flags] [files...]`
compiles only the listed `.proto` files, while keeping `--proto_path` for
import resolution. When no files are given, fall back to today's recursive
walk for backward compatibility.

## Why this exists

Every peer tool (protoc, vtproto, gogoproto) takes `.proto` file paths as
positional arguments to scope generation. Wiresmith currently has no way to
say "compile this one file" — it walks the entire `--proto_path` directory.

The visible cost lives in `Makefile:120-123`:

```make
$(eval CONF_TMP := $(shell mktemp -d))
@cp proto/conformance/test_messages_proto3.proto "$(CONF_TMP)/"
$(WIRESMITH) --proto_path="$(CONF_TMP)" --out=gen --module=$(MODULE)
@rm -rf "$(CONF_TMP)"
```

This `mktemp -d && cp` dance exists solely because wiresmith cannot be
told to compile a single file out of a multi-file directory tree. It is
the most visible smoke test for whether wiresmith's CLI ergonomics match
protoc's: when the workaround can be deleted, parity is real.

## What changes

- `cmd/wiresmith/main.go`: accept `flag.Args()` after `flag.Parse()` as
  the list of files to compile. Each argument is resolved relative to the
  current working directory (mirroring protoc).
- `compiler/generator/generator.go`: `Generator.Generate` gains the ability
  to scope compilation to a subset of files while still resolving imports
  via the full `--proto_path` walk. The internal `buildImportMapping`
  function already walks the tree; only the consumer-side "files to emit"
  filter is new.
- Empty positional args list = today's behavior (walk and emit everything).

## Acceptance criteria

- `wiresmith --proto_path=proto proto/conformance/test_messages_proto3.proto`
  emits only `test_messages_proto3.pb.go`, not the whole tree.
- The Makefile conformance step (wiresmith-DDDD; see linked ticket) can
  drop the temp-dir dance.
- No regression in `make generate-ours` for the existing directory-mode
  invocations.

## Source

Identified during 2026-05-26 review of wiresmith CLI vs. protoc/vtproto/
gogoproto. Same review that produced wiresmith-j0h, wiresmith-ahz,
wiresmith-gz4, wiresmith-vy9.
notes:
Implemented as Generator.Files filter (filesystem-path → fd.Path() map via buildImportMapping). Empty Files keeps the recursive-walk default; non-empty filters emission only — import resolution still uses the full --proto_path walk. Positional paths outside --proto_path are rejected up-front (clearer than silently emitting nothing). Makefile's conformance step dropped its mktemp -d/cp/rm dance and now invokes wiresmith with proto/conformance/test_messages_proto3.proto as a positional arg. Docs updated in docs/cli.md.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/76
close_reason: implemented: Generator.Files filter + CLI positional args; Makefile conformance step uses the new syntax

title: Publish protohelpers as a stable Go module
description:
**Source:** FLAGS.md

### 4. Publish protohelpers as a stable Go module

Move `gen/protohelpers/` out of the wiresmith generated tree and publish it
as a standalone Go module — for example `github.com/wiresmith/protohelpers`
(name/path TBD; the key property is that it's importable by any consumer
without being co-located with their generated code).

Update `compiler/generator/emit_registration.go:28` and `types.go:62` so
the generated import is the fixed published path, not `fg.module +
"/gen/protohelpers"`. The `--module` flag stops influencing the helpers
import entirely.

This is what `vtprotobuf/protohelpers`, `gogoproto/proto`, and
`google.golang.org/protobuf/runtime/...` all do — runtime support lives at
a stable, public import path.

**Note:** This duplicates the work proposed in ARCH-6 from IMPROVEMENTS.md.
notes: Blocked: requires external action (create new public Go module repo) before generator can be repointed. Releasing back to queue.

title: Drop the opentelemetry.proto.* special-case in goPackageDir
description:
**Source:** FLAGS.md

### 3. Drop the `opentelemetry.proto.*` special-case

Once `go_package` is the sole authority, the `otlp/` prefix shortcut in
`goPackageDir` is dead code. The OTLP protos already set
`option go_package = "go.opentelemetry.io/proto/otlp/common/v1"`, which
under the new model is exactly what determines their output location.
Remove the special-case branch.
notes: Blocked on wiresmith-gz4 → wiresmith-ahz (FLAGS.md steps 1+2 must land first). Removing the OTLP special-case in isolation breaks codegen: today the OTLP go_package value 'go.opentelemetry.io/proto/otlp/common/v1' is NOT under effectiveBase=wiresmith/gen, so resolveGoPackage returns ok=false and destFor falls through to goPackageDir, which uses the special-case to map opentelemetry.proto.common.v1 → otlp/common/v1. Without the special-case AND without step-2's unconditional-honor-go_package change, the OTLP files would land at gen/opentelemetry/proto/common/v1/ instead of gen/otlp/common/v1/. Defer per the FLAGS.md migration plan.
close_reason: Already fixed in code — verified during session audit (see commit message for per-bead evidence)

title: Always honor go_package; require it when imports cross packages
description:
**Source:** FLAGS.md

### 2. Always honor `go_package`; require it when imports cross packages

`resolveGoPackage` currently gates `go_package` behind "is the import path
under our `effectiveBase`?". Drop that gate. If a proto file sets
`option go_package`, that string is the generated file's import path,
unconditionally. Cross-file imports between wiresmith-generated files read
each other's `go_package` directly, the same way protoc does.

For a single-file generation with no cross-file imports, `go_package` can
be omitted and the import path is derived from `--out` + source-relative
path + the consumer's Go module root. (Or we can require `go_package`
always, matching protoc-gen-go's strictness — easier to reason about.)
notes: Scope question for next claimant: literal reading 'honor go_package unconditionally' breaks wiresmith's own OTel build, because the vendored OTel protos' go_package = 'go.opentelemetry.io/...' would become the import path but the file lives on disk at wiresmith/gen/opentelemetry/proto/.... Three paths forward to discuss with user: (a) drop the gate + add 'replace' directive in go.mod, (b) add CLI M-flag override (like vtproto/gogoproto Makefile already does at the protoc level) + drop the gate, (c) narrow to 'drop the gate when go_package matches the source-relative dest' — effectively a no-op vs. ahz's relaxed-but-keeps-build-green behavior. User input needed before claiming.
close_reason: Closed

title: Use source-relative output paths (drop proto-package-based path derivation)
description:
**Source:** FLAGS.md

### 1. Drop proto-package-based path derivation; use source-relative paths

Remove `effectiveBase` and `goPackageDir` from
`compiler/generator/names.go`. The output path becomes:

```
<--out>/<source-file's-path-relative-to---proto_path>.pb.go
```

Concretely, for input `pkg/.patched-proto/common/v1/common.proto` invoked
with `--proto_path=pkg/.patched-proto --out=pkg/tempopb`, the output is
`pkg/tempopb/common/v1/common.pb.go`. This is the `paths=source_relative`
contract.
notes:
Implementation: source-relative output path drives the on-disk location (outDir + filepath.Dir(fd.Path()) + basename). Go import-path resolution still goes through destFor with go_package honored under the legacy module/gen base; the always-honor-go_package change is wiresmith-gz4's territory. Decision: did NOT restructure vendored proto/otlp/ — instead let gen/otlp/* relocate to gen/opentelemetry/proto/* and updated 16 consumer files. Trade-off chosen so the upstream OTel protos stay byte-identical to their source.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/78
close_reason: implemented: source-relative output paths; effectiveBase and goPackageDir removed; gen/otlp/* relocated to gen/opentelemetry/proto/*; consumers updated

title: Eliminate --strip_prefix, --import_base, --helpers_import (umbrella)
description:
**Source:** FLAGS.md

# Proposal: Eliminate `--strip_prefix`, `--import_base`, `--helpers_import`

[TEMPO.md](TEMPO.md) lists three CLI flags as planned additions for the Tempo
integration:

```
--strip_prefix    Proto package prefix to strip for Go paths (e.g. "tempopb")
--import_base     Go import path base (replaces module/gen/ prefix)
--helpers_import  Import path for protohelpers package
```

Other Go protobuf compilers — `protoc-gen-go`, `protoc-gen-gogofaster`,
`protoc-gen-go-vtproto` — don't have flags like this. They get the same
configurability from two existing protoc-native mechanisms: the
`paths=source_relative` output mode and a fixed, published runtime helpers
import path. This proposal eliminates the three flags by adopting both.

## Why the flags exist

Wiresmith currently derives output locations from the proto **package**, not the
source **path**. In `compiler/generator/names.go`:

```go
func effectiveBase(module string) string {
    return module + "/gen"
}

func goPackageDir(protoPkg string) string {
    parts := strings.Split(protoPkg, ".")
    if len(parts) >= 3 && parts[0] == "opentelemetry" && parts[1] == "proto" {
        return "otlp/" + strings.Join(parts[2:], "/")
    }
    return strings.Join(parts, "/")
}
```

`destFor` builds the output directory from `effectiveBase(module) + goPackageDir(protoPkg)`,
and an `option go_package` is only honored when it falls under
`effectiveBase`. Two hardcodes are baked into this:

1. The `/gen` suffix on `effectiveBase` — needed `--import_base` to override.
2. The `opentelemetry.proto.*` → `otlp/...` special case in `goPackageDir`,
   plus the dotted-package-as-path fallback — needed `--strip_prefix` to
   re-shape for non-OTel package layouts.

The `protohelpers` import is hardcoded similarly in
`compiler/generator/emit_registration.go` and `types.go`:

```go
fg.imports.addImport(fg.module+"/gen/protohelpers", "")
```

— needed `--helpers_import` to override.

## How protoc-based plugins solve the same problem

### Output path: `paths=source_relative`

protoc-gen-go (and every plugin that wraps protoc) supports
`paths=source_relative`. The output location of a generated file is purely
`<--go_out>/<source-relative-path>.pb.go`. The proto package is *not*
consulted to compute the path.

Cross-file imports between generated files are resolved from each input
file's `option go_package`. The `go_package` is the single source of truth
for "what import path does this generated file have"; the on-disk location
just has to agree with it because Go's module layout requires it.

For Tempo this means: a patched proto at
`pkg/.patched-proto/common/v1/common.proto` with
`option go_package = "github.com/grafana/tempo/pkg/tempopb/common/v1";` and
`--go_out=pkg/tempopb --go_opt=paths=source_relative` produces
`pkg/tempopb/common/v1/common.pb.go`. No `gen/` in the path, no per-package
flags. The proto file's own metadata fully specifies the layout.

### Runtime helpers: fixed module path

vtprotobuf publishes its runtime support at the stable Go module path
`github.com/planetscale/vtprotobuf/protohelpers`. Generated code imports
that path verbatim. There is no per-codegen flag for "where do helpers
live" because the answer is always the same: at the published module path.

Consumers don't vendor helpers under their own tree; they take a Go module
dependency like any other library.

## Migration path for existing wiresmith consumers

The wiresmith codebase itself uses `--module=wiresmith --out=gen` and
relies on `effectiveBase = "wiresmith/gen"` plus the OTel special-case to
produce `gen/otlp/common/v1/common.pb.go` from
`package opentelemetry.proto.common.v1`. Under this proposal, those protos
need `option go_package = "wiresmith/gen/otlp/common/v1";` (or wherever
wiresmith wants them to land) and invocation drops `--module`.

This is a one-time edit per generated proto package in the wiresmith repo,
done at the same commit that lands the codegen change. After that, the
codegen layer has no module-level configuration at all.

## Resulting consumer experience

Tempo's invocation becomes:

```bash
wiresmith \
  --proto_path=pkg/.patched-proto \
  --out=pkg/tempopb
```

— with each patched proto setting its `option go_package` to
`github.com/grafana/tempo/pkg/tempopb/<X>/v1` (which Tempo's `gen-proto`
make target already does for the gogofast pass). The `--module`,
`--strip_prefix`, `--import_base`, and `--helpers_import` flags all
disappear; the only remaining flags are `--proto_path` and `--out`, both of
which match protoc's `--proto_path` / `--go_out` and need no explanation
to anyone who has used protoc before.

Generated files land at `pkg/tempopb/common/v1/common.pb.go` directly —
no `gen/` subdirectory and no 101-file import rewrite of Tempo consumers,
which is the diff-pollution cost of the current workaround documented in
[TEMPO.md](TEMPO.md).
notes:
## Strategic context (added 2026-05-26)

This umbrella simplifies the standalone `cmd/wiresmith` CLI. The BUF-EPIC
(wiresmith-c4r) is a parallel track adding `cmd/protoc-gen-wiresmith` as a
protoc plugin. Implementers should be aware that two surfaces will coexist
unless/until a decision is made to deprecate one:

- Today: `cmd/wiresmith` is the only entry point.
- After this umbrella lands: standalone CLI matches protoc flag idioms
  (paths=source_relative, go_package as truth) but is still
  directory-walking, not plugin-style.
- After BUF-EPIC lands: `cmd/protoc-gen-wiresmith` exists alongside, usable
  from `buf generate`. Standalone CLI is still maintained.
- Possible future: deprecate standalone CLI once plugin is stable; the
  plugin route is what all peer tools (protoc-gen-go, vtproto, gogoproto)
  expose exclusively.

Do not make changes in this umbrella that would prevent the plugin track,
and vice versa. The generator core (descriptor-in, files-out) should remain
the shared substrate for both surfaces.

title: DOC-4: TEMPO.md documents flags that do not exist
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** `TEMPO.md:6-23`.

**What:** Promises `--strip_prefix`, `--import_base`, `--helpers_import`. None exist in `cmd/wiresmith/main.go`. `FLAGS.md` (untracked) proposes *not* adding them. Users following TEMPO.md will see `flag provided but not defined: -strip_prefix`.

**Fix:** Either commit `FLAGS.md` and link to it as the resolution, or rewrite `TEMPO.md` to say "proposed; superseded by FLAGS.md". Don't leave contradictory truth claims in the tree.
close_reason: resolved by deleting TEMPO.md — file removed entirely instead of patched. Dependency on j0h was originally because z9k would auto-resolve when the flag-elimination umbrella landed; now resolved earlier via deletion. j0h remains open and continues independent of TEMPO.md.

title: DOC-3: (wiresmith.options.pointer) field option undocumented
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** missing from `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `MIMIR.md`, `TEMPO.md`, `BUF.md`, `FLAGS.md`.

**What:** PR #59 added the public extension option. Users must `import "wiresmith/options.proto"` in their own protos. Only references today are the test fixture (`proto/basic/pointer.proto`) and a source comment in `compiler/generator/embed/wiresmith/options.proto`. CLAUDE.md "Keep documentation in sync" caveat applies directly.

**Fix:** Add "Custom options" section to README and `AGENTS.md` "Design decisions" with: import path, no-vendoring guarantee, syntax (`(wiresmith.options.pointer) = true`), what it does (`*T`/`[]*T`), where it's invalid (optional/oneof/map/scalar).
notes:
Added 'Custom field options' section to AGENTS.md pointing at docs/extensions.md; updated MIMIR.md 'Pointer semantics' entry to show (gogoproto.nullable) has shipped as (wiresmith.options.pointer). README.md already linked docs/extensions.md (PR #71). docs/extensions.md was already comprehensive; TEMPO/BUF/FLAGS.md are not about field-level options so skipped.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/73
close_reason: merged via #73

title: SEC-5: Cross-package message unmarshal resets recursion-depth counter
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go:167-169` (public `Unmarshal` wrapper) + `compiler/types/type.go:236-242` (`emitUnmarshalCall`).

**What:** Same-package nested messages call `.unmarshal(b, depth+1)`; cross-package nested messages call `.Unmarshal(b)` which restarts depth at 0. A graph bouncing between packages can recurse to `maxUnmarshalDepth × pkgCount` levels. For OTel today the package count is small enough to be safe, but the protection silently weakens as packages multiply. Documented contract ("unmarshal depth is bounded") is broken across the boundary.

**Fix:** Give every message a single internal entry like `unmarshalWithDepth(b, depth)` and have cross-package callers invoke it via an exported wrapper that accepts the counter, or wrap depth in a context-style parameter. Concrete sketch: emit `UnmarshalWithDepth(b []byte, depth int) error` as the cross-package surface and call it from `emitUnmarshalCall` when `!isSamePackage`.
notes:
Implemented: emit a public UnmarshalWithDepth(b, depth) method on every generated message; cross-package unmarshal sites now route through it with depth+1 instead of the depth-resetting public Unmarshal(b). Three call sites changed: emit_unmarshal.go's oneof-message and optional-message branches, plus the shared emitUnmarshalCall helper in compiler/types/type.go. TestGenerateCrossPackageUnmarshalThreadsDepth pins the codegen contract; TestUnmarshalWithDepthRespectsStartingDepth pins the runtime contract. Documented in docs/generated-api.md. All 696 conformance tests still pass (regenerated gen/ + ran tests; no diff in gen/ output beyond the new method).
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/77
close_reason: implemented: cross-package unmarshal now threads depth via UnmarshalWithDepth

title: TEST-3: Add test for field number 0 rejection in unmarshal
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** missing — should live under `test/basic/wire_validation_test.go`.

**What:** `type.go:169` emits `if X>>3 < 1 || X>>3 > 0x1FFFFFFF { return ... }`. CLAUDE.md lists this as a recurring caveat. A regression would be silent today.

**Fix:** One table-driven test: feed `[]byte{0x00, ...}` (tag = field 0, wire type 0) through every constructor in `testutil.AllMessageConstructors()` and assert a non-nil error containing "invalid field number".
notes:
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/74
Implementation note: switched from AllMessageConstructors() (per the original Fix text) to AllPanicSafeConstructors() in response to PR #74 review — the test asserts only on the returned error, so map-bearing types are safe to include and bring the actual coverage up to the documented 'every generated Unmarshal' claim.
close_reason: PR #74 merged (commit 099309d on main: 'test: pin field-0 tag rejection across every generated Unmarshal'); status was open due to a sync miss

title: DOC-2: README Usage omits install + actual CLI invocation
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** `README.md:88-97`.

**What:** Only "Usage" content is `make generate/build/test/bench`. No `go install`, no example `wiresmith --proto_path=... --out=...`, no mention of `--version` or `--help`. Reader cannot use the binary outside this repo's Makefile.

**Fix:** Add "Install" section with `go install wiresmith/cmd/wiresmith@latest` and "Run" with the three real flags. Reference `--help` and `--version`.
notes:
Implementation deviates from bead's suggested 'go install wiresmith/cmd/wiresmith@latest' because the project's module path is bare 'wiresmith' (no host prefix) — go install would try to fetch 'wiresmith' as a remote module and fail. Followed docs/cli.md instead: git clone + go build.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/79
close_reason: implemented: README now has Install (build-from-source) and Run sections with the real CLI invocation; --help and --version referenced

title: CR-6: Size() and Marshal* panic on nil; Get*/Equal are nil-safe
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_size.go:15`; `emit_marshal.go:17,27,34`.

**What:** Inconsistent nil-safety contract. Users who learn "wiresmith methods are nil-safe" from `Get*`/`Has*`/`Equal`/`Reset`/`String` will be surprised when `nil.Size()` panics. Either tighten the contract universally or document the cutoff explicitly.

**Fix:** Emit `if m == nil { return 0 }` for `Size()` and `if m == nil { return nil, nil }` (or `(0, nil)`) for the `Marshal*` family. Cost: one branch in cold path. Alternative: explicitly document `Size`/`Marshal` as nil-unsafe in CLAUDE.md.
close_reason: Already shipped in merged PRs #68, #72, #69, #67, #70 respectively

title: SEC-4: Inline varint decoder lacks 10-byte boundary overflow check
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** `compiler/types/type.go:174-185` (`emitConsumeVarintAt`), `216-232` (`emitConsumeBytesLenAt`); skip path in `emit_unmarshal.go:31-40`.

**What:** The loop terminates on `shift >= 64` (next iteration), so iteration 10 still runs with `shift == 63` and does `v |= uint64(b & 0x7F) << 63`. Bits 1–6 of the 10th byte silently overflow past the uint64 boundary. The wire-format spec requires that on the 10th byte, `b & 0x7F` MUST be ≤ 1. CLAUDE.md flags this exact requirement. The tag decoder (line 157-170) is fine — its 5-byte cap + field-number range check at line 169 cover it.

**Fix:** After reading `b` inside the loop, emit `if shift == 63 && b > 1 { return fmt.Errorf("proto: varint overflow") }`. Apply to both `emitConsumeVarintAt` and `emitConsumeBytesLenAt`. Cheap; one branch in the cold path.
close_reason: Already shipped in merged PRs #68, #72, #69, #67, #70 respectively

title: TEST-2: Add unit tests for 22 untested compiler/types/*.go files
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** missing — `compiler/types/{bool,bytes,double,float,fixed*,sint*,int*,uint*,varint,map,message,oneof,optional,pointer,repeated*,string}_test.go`.

**What:** Only `equal_test.go` exists. The other `Type` interface methods (`EmitSize`, `EmitMarshal`, `EmitUnmarshal`, `EmitValueSize`, `EmitValueMarshal`, `CastExpr`, `IsPackable`, `WireType`, `SizeByIndex`, `FixedSize`, `VarintSizeExpr`, `ZeroLiteral`) are per-kind invariants that should be locked down individually.

**Fix:** Extend the `captureEmitter` pattern from `equal_test.go`. Highest-value cases:
  - `bytes_test.go`: confirms `EmitUnmarshal` uses `append([]byte(nil), ...)` (bytes-aliasing guard).
  - `map_test.go`: confirms merge-of-empty-but-present message; confirms key/value tag ordering.
  - `oneof_test.go`: confirms `EmitSize` BytesType/FixedSize branches.
  - `fixed64_test.go` / `fixed32_test.go`: confirm signbit-aware emit after CR-2.
notes:
Added unit tests for all per-kind type files in compiler/types/ (18 new _test.go files, ~135 test cases). Pattern follows equal_test.go's captureEmitter. Coverage: 84.1% of compiler/types statements. Locked down highest-value invariants: bytes-aliasing guard in EmitMapEntryUnmarshal, map merge-on-present-not-len, oneof EmitSize FixedSize/BytesType/fallthrough branches, signbit-aware EmitMarshal/EmitSize for float/double (CR-2), bit-exact Equal for float/double (CR-5).
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/88
close_reason: implemented

title: PERF-3: Strip redundant m.Size() call from generated MarshalTo
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** `compiler/types/repeated.go:23-34` and generator-side equivalent.

**What:** `MarshalTo` is meant for caller-allocated buffers — the caller already sized them. Calling `m.Size()` again walks the entire message tree a second time; for nested OTel messages that's the same O(N) work done twice. Bench harness doesn't currently isolate this (`bench/ours_bench_test.go:14-22` always calls the size-allocating `Marshal()`), masking the regression.

**Fix:** Strip the internal `m.Size()` from generated `MarshalTo`. Document the precondition: `len(dAtA) >= m.Size()`. Add `BenchmarkMarshalTracesPreallocated_*` variants that pre-allocate the buffer outside the timer.

**Validate:** Expect ~30% improvement on the new pre-allocated marshal benchmarks.
close_reason: wontfix

title: DOC-1: README scalar list incomplete vs AGENTS.md
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** `README.md:84`; cross-check `AGENTS.md:63`.

**What:** README lists `string, bool, int32, int64, uint32, uint64, sint32, double, bytes, fixed32, fixed64, sfixed64`. Missing `sint64`, `float`, `sfixed32`. Code in `compiler/types/{sfixed32,sint64,float}.go` confirms support. README also omits maps entirely (line 82) despite full support.

**Fix:** Sync README scalar list to AGENTS.md. Add maps to the feature list.
close_reason: README supported proto3 feature list now includes maps and the full supported scalar/map type set.

title: CR-5: Equal() returns false for two identical NaN values
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** `compiler/types/fixed64.go:96-98` and `fixed32.go:96-98` — `scalarNotEqualGuard` emits `if lhs != rhs { return false }`. For floats, `NaN != NaN == true`.

**What:** Two messages with identical NaN payloads compare unequal, while `proto.Equal` from the official library treats them as equal. This is a known divergence with gogoproto, but wiresmith's `Equal()` isn't documented as such. The same code path also makes the contract awkward once CR-2 lands (signbit-aware zero).

**Fix:** Emit `if math.Float64bits(lhs) != math.Float64bits(rhs)` (resp. `Float32bits`). Bit-exact equality kills NaN-asymmetry and negative-zero edge cases in one stroke. Document the chosen contract in `AGENTS.md`.
notes: PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/85
close_reason: Already fixed in code — verified during session audit (see commit message for per-bead evidence)

title: ARCH-1: Move message-shaped unmarshal logic onto Type interface
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go:210-330` (~120 lines, five nested decision branches).

**What:** Three `case protoreflect.MessageKind` blocks at lines 269-283, 295-309, 321-326 re-implement "consume length-delimited, allocate or reuse pointer/value, call Unmarshal" with subtle differences (oneof reuses-or-replaces; optional `new()`s; pointer-option goes through `PointerField`). The recent move of equality into `Type` (commit `428fce8`) did not get applied here.

**Fix:** Add `EmitOneofVariantUnmarshal(e, ooField, variantName, fieldName, ctx)` to `Type`. Make `OneofField` a composite wrapping `Inner`, analogous to `OptionalField`/`PointerField`. `emitFieldUnmarshal` then becomes ~30 lines of pure dispatch.
notes:
Implemented in branch bd-wiresmith-qks-unmarshal-message-composites. Two structural changes:

1. OneofField gains OneofName/VariantName/FieldName + working EmitUnmarshal (was panic). Handles all three inner kinds: MessageKind reuse-or-replace merge, length-delimited (string/bytes), and value types (varint/fixed).

2. Optional message routed through PointerField (same *Msg shape and lazy-allocate semantics as the pointer-option case). Removed the hand-rolled lazy-allocate block from emit_unmarshal.go.

Three MessageKind hand-rolled blocks reduced to zero. emit_unmarshal.go's emitFieldUnmarshal lost the inline merge + lazy-allocate code. Codegen byte-identical to main (verified via make generate-ours + git diff gen/ -- only the pre-existing protoc v7.35.0 vs v7.34.1 version-comment skew remained, reverted as in #88-followups).

Tests: replaced TestOneofField_EmitUnmarshal_Panics with positive coverage (value-type, string variant, message variant same-package, message variant cross-package).
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/90
close_reason: implemented

title: PERF-2: Add 1-byte fast-path to tag decode
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** `compiler/types/type.go:157-170` (`EmitConsumeTagAt`); fires every iteration of the main parse loop.

**What:** 95%+ of OTel proto fields use field numbers 1-15 (single-byte tag, high bit clear). The generic varint loop pays loop setup + two bounds checks per byte even on the single-byte path.

**Fix:** Emit a peel: `if iNdEx < l && dAtA[iNdEx] < 0x80 { wire = uint64(dAtA[iNdEx]); iNdEx++ } else { /* generic loop */ }`. Same trick for `byteLen` decode when length < 128 (short strings, small nested messages). Neither vtproto nor gogoproto does this — clean differentiator.

**Validate:** Microbench on `UnmarshalSingleSpan` should improve 10-20%.
notes:
Implementation: peeled 1-byte fast-path on both EmitConsumeTagAt and emitConsumeBytesLenAt (bead said tag + 'same trick for byteLen'). Field-number range check after the if/else catches the byte<8 case so no extra validation in fast-path.

Bench (count=20, 1s, Apple M4 Pro):
  UnmarshalSingleSpan_Ours  -4.63% (target)
  UnmarshalLogs_Ours        -5.22%
  UnmarshalSum_Ours         -4.82%
  UnmarshalProfiles_Ours    -4.68%
  UnmarshalGauge_Ours       -4.52%
  UnmarshalHistogram_Ours   -3.94%
  UnmarshalExpHistogram_Ours -3.78%
  UnmarshalSummary_Ours     -2.90%
  UnmarshalTraces_Ours      -1.55%
  UnmarshalMap_Ours         -0.77%
  geomean                   -3.39%
Noise controls: MarshalSingleSpan +0.85%, SizeTraces -4.54% (SizeTraces moved on layout shift alone — codegen shape change cascades into SSA decisions). All targeted moves p<0.01 and consistent in direction; bead predicted 10-20%, achieved ~5% — the surrounding setup/struct-fill cost was already a meaningful fraction of total.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/91
close_reason: 1-byte tag and byteLen fast-paths added; UnmarshalSingleSpan -4.63%, unmarshal geomean -3.39% across 10 benchmarks (p<0.01)

title: TEST-1: Add per-emit_*.go unit tests for generator phases
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** missing — should live under `compiler/generator/emit_*_test.go`.

**What:** Of 11 emit files (~1067 LOC), only `emit_equal.go` has unit-level coverage, and that test sits in the wrong package because `EmitEqual` is on the `Type` interface. Every other emit phase — has, getter, reset, struct, marshal, size, unmarshal, oneof, registration, enum — is only exercised transitively via the round-trip `TestGenerateMatchesCheckedIn` smoke check. When that fails, you cannot localise to a phase.

**Fix:** Build a small test harness in `compiler/generator/testutil_test.go` that captures `body strings.Builder` against a synthetic `protoreflect.MessageDescriptor`. Add one table-driven file per emit:
  - `emit_has_test.go`: 0 / 1 / 65 presence fields; pointer-option excluded; bitmap word count.
  - `emit_oneof_test.go`: variant interface method name; scalar/bytes/message payload switch; single-variant oneof.
  - `emit_registration_test.go`: empty file → no `init()`; only enums; `allow_alias` enum dedup.
  - `emit_unmarshal_test.go`: wire-type mismatch dispatch; pre-scan emission threshold.
  - `emit_reset_test.go`: pin the nil-guard from CR-1 once it lands.
close_reason: Closed by PR #92 / commit 7be9ea2 — added testutil_test.go harness plus emit_{has,oneof,registration,reset,unmarshal}_test.go

title: PERF-1: Use inline decoders in packed-scalar unmarshal hot loop
description:
**Severity:** High

**Location:** `compiler/types/repeated.go:184-211` (packed-decode emit). The non-packed path already uses `emitConsumeFixed64At` / `emitConsumeVarintAt` (the inline decoders); the packed path falls back to `protowire.ConsumeFixed64(data)` + `data = data[vn:]`.

**What:** Inconsistency penalises exactly the hot path: OTel histogram `bucket_counts` and `explicit_bounds` are packed `fixed64`/`uint64` slices iterated thousands of times per second. The reslice `data = data[vn:]` is cheap but not free; the function-call boundary inhibits BCE.

**Fix:** Emit `emitConsumeFixed{32,64}At` (or `emitConsumeVarintAt`) in the packed inner loop, indexing against `postIndex` directly. Same machinery, same correctness, ~5-15% expected speedup on `UnmarshalHistogram`/`UnmarshalExpHistogram`.

**Validate:** `make bench-compare COUNT=-count=10` on the histogram benches.
close_reason: packed-varint inlined; -8.4% on UnmarshalExpHistogram, -31% on packed-varint micro

title: Emit XXX_* methods + XXX_unrecognized field (gogo proto.Buffer compat)
description:
**Source:** MIMIR.md "Gogo method set". User confirmed Mimir uses this 2026-05-29.

**What gogo does:** emits a set of deprecated-but-used internal methods that satisfy the older `proto.Marshaler`/`proto.Buffer` interface shape. Required by older gogo/Mimir code that hasn't been updated to the modern `proto.Marshal` API.

**Methods to emit (per message):**
- `XXX_Marshal(b []byte, deterministic bool) ([]byte, error)` — wraps `m.MarshalToSizedBuffer`.
- `XXX_Unmarshal(b []byte) error` — wraps `m.Unmarshal`.
- `XXX_Size() int` — wraps `m.Size`.
- `XXX_Merge(src proto.Message)` — merge src into m via marshal+unmarshal (or via a smarter per-field merge).
- `XXX_DiscardUnknown()` — no-op (wiresmith already discards unknown fields on unmarshal per design).
- `XXX_unrecognized []byte` field — this is the tricky one: it's a struct field, not a method. Used by older gogo to roundtrip unknown fields. **Decision needed:** either emit the field as always-nil (preserving the API shape but not the behavior, since wiresmith discards unknown fields by design — `docs/design.md:34`), or gate this whole bead behind a flag `--gogo-compat-xxx` so users opting in accept the surface without the behavior.

**Recommendation:** gate behind a flag (`--emit-gogo-xxx` or option). XXX_* are deprecated; consumers who need them should opt in explicitly. Default off.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- All XXX_* methods compile and pass through to existing implementations.
- `XXX_unrecognized` field is always nil after Unmarshal (consistent with the "unknown fields discarded" design decision).
- Test that an older gogo-style consumer (`proto.Buffer{}`) successfully Marshal/Unmarshal a wiresmith message via XXX_* methods.
- Document the deprecation status and the unknown-field caveat in `docs/generated-api.md` and `docs/extensions.md`.

**Note for future deletion:** XXX_* are deprecated in gogo. Once Mimir modernizes its proto integration, this bead's output can be removed. Consider scoping the emission behind a flag from day one to make removal painless.
notes:
**File layout decision:** XXX_* methods (and the XXX_unrecognized field) MUST be emitted to a separate file per source proto: `<name>_gogo_compat.pb.go` (sibling to `<name>.pb.go`). Rationale: (1) keeps the deprecated gogo-compat surface clearly separated and auditable; (2) makes removal trivial when Mimir modernizes — delete the file, regenerate, done; (3) lets us gate emission on the `--emit-gogo-xxx` flag (or per-file option) by simply not writing the second file when off; (4) matches the protoc-gen-go-grpc precedent of `<name>_grpc.pb.go` co-existing in the same Go package.

**XXX_unrecognized caveat:** since it's a struct field, it cannot live in a separate file — fields must be declared with the struct in `<name>.pb.go`. Options: (a) drop the field entirely (XXX_unrecognized is always-nil in wiresmith anyway because unknown fields are discarded per design); (b) emit the field unconditionally in `<name>.pb.go` but only emit XXX_* methods in `<name>_gogo_compat.pb.go`. Recommend (a) — the field has no behavior anyway, and emitting it forces every consumer to see the stub. Document that gogo consumers checking `m.XXX_unrecognized != nil` to detect unknown-field presence will need to migrate; that check is meaningless on wiresmith messages.

title: Emit GoString() method per generated message (gogo-style)
description:
**Source:** MIMIR.md "Gogo method set". User confirmed Mimir uses this 2026-05-29.

**What gogo does:** emits `func (m *Foo) GoString() string` returning a Go-syntax representation that round-trips through `fmt.Sprintf("%#v", m)`. Distinct from `String()` (text proto format). Used by Mimir for debugging output and golden-file test fixtures.

**Proposed wiresmith shape:**
- New generator emit phase: `emit_gostring.go`.
- For each generated message, emit:
  ```go
  func (m *Foo) GoString() string {
      if m == nil { return "(*Foo)(nil)" }
      s := make([]string, 0, N)
      s = append(s, "&pkg.Foo{")
      s = append(s, fmt.Sprintf("FieldA: %#v,", m.FieldA))
      ...
      s = append(s, "}")
      return strings.Join(s, " ")
  }
  ```
- Handle oneofs by GoString-ing the active variant struct.
- Handle maps and repeated by GoString-ing the slice/map literal.
- Skip unset fields (matches gogo behavior — only emit fields that are present per the bitmap).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Round-trip test: `fmt.Sprintf("%#v", &foo)` is parseable Go (compile-check the output as a string in a test).
- Nil-safe (returns `"(*Foo)(nil)"`).
- Unit test in `compiler/generator/emit_gostring_test.go`.
- Update `AGENTS.md` "Generated API surface" and `docs/generated-api.md` to list `GoString()` alongside `String()`.

title: Add (wiresmith.options.stdtime) — google.protobuf.Timestamp as time.Time
description:
**Source:** MIMIR.md "Well-known type helpers". Confirmed needed by user 2026-05-29.

**What gogo does:** `(gogoproto.stdtime) = true` on a `google.protobuf.Timestamp` field emits a `time.Time` field directly (instead of `*timestamppb.Timestamp`), with helpers that convert seconds + nanos to/from `time.Time`. Mimir uses this on sample timestamps, query time ranges, etc.

**Distinct from BUF-2.2 (`wiresmith-7m6`):** parallel to stdduration — that issue gives structural `Timestamp` support; this gives the ergonomic `time.Time` substitution.

**Proposed wiresmith shape:**
- Add `(wiresmith.options.stdtime) = true` to `compiler/generator/embed/wiresmith/options.proto`.
- Detect on Timestamp-typed fields; reject elsewhere.
- Emit `Foo time.Time`.
- Marshal: `t.Unix()` → seconds, `t.Nanosecond()` → nanos, encode as length-delimited Timestamp sub-message.
- Unmarshal: read seconds + nanos, `time.Unix(seconds, int64(nanos)).UTC()`. UTC is the protobuf canonical timezone; document.
- Be careful about the zero value: `time.Time{}` is January 1, year 1 UTC. proto3 default semantics treat unset Timestamp as Unix epoch zero. Pick one and document — gogo treats zero `time.Time` as "not set" via separate presence; wiresmith should follow.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `proto/basic/stdtime.proto` fixture (shared with stdduration) with a Timestamp field.
- Round-trip: now-ish times, zero, far-past (year 1), far-future (year 9999). Cross-library check vs official `timestamppb.Timestamp`.
- Decide and document the zero-value/presence semantics; lock it down with a test.
- Update `docs/extensions.md`.

title: Add (wiresmith.options.stdduration) — google.protobuf.Duration as time.Duration
description:
**Source:** MIMIR.md "Well-known type helpers". Confirmed needed by user 2026-05-29.

**What gogo does:** `(gogoproto.stdduration) = true` on a `google.protobuf.Duration` field emits a `time.Duration` field directly in the struct (instead of `*durationpb.Duration`), with marshal/unmarshal helpers that convert seconds + nanos to/from `time.Duration`. Mimir uses this for retention windows, lookback durations, query timeouts.

**Distinct from BUF-2.2 (`wiresmith-7m6`):** that issue covers structural WKT support — making `google.protobuf.Duration` work as a regular nested message. This issue is the *ergonomic* alternative: when the user opts in, the field becomes `time.Duration` directly, bypassing the `Duration` struct entirely. Both should be available.

**Proposed wiresmith shape:**
- Add `(wiresmith.options.stdduration) = true` to `compiler/generator/embed/wiresmith/options.proto`.
- Generator detects the option on a field whose type is `google.protobuf.Duration` (reject elsewhere) and emits `Foo time.Duration` instead.
- Marshal: encode `time.Duration` → seconds + nanos as a length-delimited Duration sub-message.
- Unmarshal: decode the sub-message and convert seconds + nanos back to `time.Duration` (saturating on overflow — `time.Duration` is int64 nanos, while proto allows up to ~10000 years; document the truncation behavior).
- Import `time` only when the option appears in the file.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `proto/basic/stdtime.proto` (shared fixture with stdtime bead) with a Duration field annotated.
- Round-trip test covers normal values, zero, negative, and the truncation boundary.
- Cross-library check: encode with wiresmith, decode with official protobuf into a `*durationpb.Duration` — values match.
- Document migration ergonomics (gogoproto alias) under `docs/extensions.md`.

title: Add (wiresmith.options.casttype) field option (gogo casttype equivalent)
description:
**Source:** MIMIR.md "Pointer semantics" section. Confirmed needed by user 2026-05-29.

**What gogo does:** `(gogoproto.casttype) = "MyAlias"` casts the generated field to a different Go type name *without* changing the wire encoding. Used for type-safe int aliases like `type MetricType int32`, distinguishing IDs by domain (`type UserID int64`, `type AccountID int64`), etc. Unlike customtype, the wire-level kind stays exactly what proto says — only the Go-side name changes.

**Proposed wiresmith shape:**
- Add `(wiresmith.options.casttype) = "MyAlias"` to `compiler/generator/embed/wiresmith/options.proto`.
- Generator emits `Foo MyAlias` instead of `Foo int64` (or whatever the proto kind would imply). All marshal/unmarshal/size code wraps the underlying kind in a cast (`int64(m.Foo)`, `MyAlias(v)` on read).
- The aliased type must be a Go `type X = Underlying` (alias) or `type X Underlying` (defined type). Both work because the wire encoding stays unchanged.
- Restrict to scalar kinds (int32/int64/uint32/uint64/sint32/sint64/fixed*/sfixed*/float/double/bool/string/bytes); reject on message/map/oneof/enum.

**Migration ergonomics:** same as customtype — consider accepting `(gogoproto.casttype)` as an alias.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `proto/basic/casttype.proto` fixture: cast int64 → custom alias, cast string → custom alias, cast bytes → custom alias.
- Test in `test/basic/casttype_test.go` covering round-trip + Equal + cross-library (wiresmith ↔ official protobuf, since wire format is unchanged).
- Reject on disallowed kinds with a clear error.
- Update `docs/extensions.md`.

title: Add (wiresmith.options.customtype) field option (gogo customtype equivalent)
description:
**Source:** MIMIR.md "Pointer semantics" section; Mimir uses gogo's `(gogoproto.customtype) = "X.Y"` extensively for types like `LabelAdapter`, custom UUID/Decimal types, etc. Confirmed needed by user 2026-05-29.

**What gogo does:** `(gogoproto.customtype) = "github.com/foo/bar.MyType"` replaces the generated Go field type with the user-supplied type. The user-supplied type must implement gogo's `Marshaler`/`Unmarshaler` shape so that wire encoding works. The field is emitted as `Foo MyType` instead of `Foo []byte` (or whatever the proto kind would imply).

**Proposed wiresmith shape:**
- Add `(wiresmith.options.customtype) = "github.com/foo/bar.MyType"` to `compiler/generator/embed/wiresmith/options.proto`.
- Generator emits `Foo MyType` and routes Marshal/Unmarshal through `MyType.MarshalWiresmith()`/`UnmarshalWiresmith(b []byte) error` (or the gogo shape — pick one, document it).
- Reject on fields where the proto kind cannot round-trip through the custom type's encoding (e.g. customtype on a message field needs different plumbing than on bytes).

**Migration ergonomics:** consider also accepting `(gogoproto.customtype)` as a recognized alias so Mimir doesn't need to rewrite every annotation. Decide during implementation; document the choice.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Add `proto/basic/customtype.proto` fixture with at least: customtype on bytes, customtype on string, customtype on a nested message. Add a Go type for each that satisfies the chosen interface.
- Test in `test/basic/customtype_test.go` covering round-trip + Equal.
- Reject customtype on map/oneof/repeated with a clear error (matches the existing `(wiresmith.options.pointer)` rejection style).
- Update `docs/extensions.md` to document the new option.

title: Verify protoc-gen-go-grpc compatibility with wiresmith messages
description:
**Goal:** Empirically validate that `protoc-gen-go-grpc`-generated client/server stubs interoperate end-to-end with wiresmith-generated message types. If verification passes, native gRPC stub generation (wiresmith-36b umbrella + GRPC-1..4 children) becomes unnecessary.

**Hypothesis (informs the test plan):**

1. wiresmith messages register a `protoimpl.MessageInfo` fast-path methods table (`docs/design.md:29`).
2. `google.golang.org/grpc/encoding/proto` calls `proto.Marshal(msg)` which dispatches via the methods table → wiresmith's hand-rolled Marshal. No reflection slow-path on the gRPC hot path.
3. `protoc-gen-go-grpc` honors `option go_package`, so wiresmith and protoc-gen-go-grpc can emit into the same Go package (`<name>.pb.go` and `<name>_grpc.pb.go` — different files, no symbol collision).

**Test plan:**

1. Add `proto/basic/service.proto` with: 1 unary RPC, 1 client-stream, 1 server-stream, 1 bidi RPC. Each carrying real OTLP-ish payloads (not empty messages).
2. Wire `make generate-ours` to invoke wiresmith for messages AND `protoc-gen-go-grpc --go-grpc_out=gen --go-grpc_opt=paths=source_relative` for stubs, targeting the same output root.
3. Add `test/peer/grpc_protoc_compat_test.go`:
   - In-process `grpc.NewServer` registered via the protoc-gen-go-grpc-emitted `RegisterFooServer`.
   - Client via the emitted `NewFooClient(conn)`.
   - Round-trip all 4 streaming modes; assert wire-level equality with the wiresmith Equal method.
   - Sanity-check `grpc.NewServer().GetServiceInfo()` lists the wiresmith services (uses GlobalFiles registry).
4. Benchmark: compare per-RPC latency of (wiresmith + protoc-gen-go-grpc) vs (protoc-gen-go + protoc-gen-go-grpc) on the same .proto. Expect wiresmith side to be faster on Marshal/Unmarshal (fast-path methods table), parity-or-slightly-slower on call setup.

**Pass criteria (ALL must hold):**

- Compile cleanly with both code generators emitting into the same package.
- All 4 streaming modes round-trip without error.
- Server reflection (`grpc.reflection.v1.ServerReflection`) lists the wiresmith services.
- Bench shows no regression vs the protoc-gen-go baseline; Marshal/Unmarshal columns should show wiresmith's usual wins.

**Outcome paths:**

- **PASS** → close `wiresmith-36b` + GRPC-1..4 (`1w4`, `nyd`, `dxi`, `3q1`) as superseded with reason "protoc-gen-go-grpc reuse verified — see <this issue>". Add a "Using wiresmith with gRPC" section to README/docs explaining the recipe. Net effort: ~200 LOC test + docs vs ~1500 LOC of new emit code.
- **PARTIAL FAIL** → identify the specific gap (e.g. a gRPC interceptor that calls field-level reflection and panics). If the gap is fixable in wiresmith in <1 day, file a follow-up bead and fix it; then this verification re-runs. The umbrella stays blocked.
- **HARD FAIL** → keep `wiresmith-36b` umbrella scope; this bead closes as "investigated, native gen required". Document the blocker in the umbrella so future contributors know why we chose the heavier path.

**Why P2:** Same priority as the umbrella it gates — a fast verification is on the critical path for Mimir integration whichever way it resolves.
notes: Dependents (wiresmith-36b umbrella + GRPC-1..4 children: 1w4, nyd, dxi, 3q1) MUST be closed as superseded if this verification passes. The umbrella + children represent ~1500 LOC of new emit code; verification + docs is ~200 LOC. Reuse-first is the strict win unless a concrete gap surfaces during testing. Whoever closes this bead with PASS should run `bd close wiresmith-36b wiresmith-1w4 wiresmith-nyd wiresmith-dxi wiresmith-3q1 --reason="superseded by <this-id>: protoc-gen-go-grpc reuse verified"` in one command.

title: Multi --proto_path support (compile across multiple proto roots)
description:
**Source:** MIMIR.md "Multi-path proto resolution" (lines 43-44) — Mimir integration needs `ProtoPaths []string` and `ProtoFiles []string` on `Generator` plus `multiPathResolver` for cross-directory proto imports.

**Why:** Today wiresmith's CLI accepts a single `--proto_path` directory and walks it recursively (`compiler/generator/generator.go:buildImportMapping`). Mimir's proto layout uses multiple roots (vendor/, internal/, etc.) — needs `-I=root1 -I=root2` style like `protoc`. The current single-root walker forces consumers to either symlink/copy or use Tempo's `.patched-proto` workaround. Mimir integration is on the near-term roadmap (confirmed 2026-05-29) so this is unblocking work.

**Approach:**
- Allow repeated `--proto_path=` flags (matches `protoc -I=path1 -I=path2`); colon-separated alternative also acceptable.
- In `buildImportMapping`, walk each root and union the results, keying by the unresolved import path (e.g. `common/v1/foo.proto`), not the absolute path.
- When an `import "foo.proto"` is resolved, check roots in order; first match wins (matches `protoc`). Document the precedence rule.
- Collision policy: if the same import path resolves to different absolute paths across roots, fail loudly with both candidate paths in the error — silent shadowing is the bug class we want to avoid.

**Interaction with existing closed work:**
- `wiresmith-7eh` (positional `.proto` paths) already lets users scope emission to specific files — keep that. After this bead, the positional paths still must live under *some* `--proto_path` root (any of them).
- `wiresmith-j0h` umbrella (source-relative output paths) is orthogonal — source-relative computation needs to pick the *matching* root per file, which falls out naturally if `buildImportMapping` retains the root each file came from.

**Out of scope:**
- Virtual filesystem (reading from tarball / module cache).
- Buf-style module resolution (covered by BUF-EPIC `wiresmith-c4r`).
- Auto-resolving `google/protobuf/*` from a built-in well-known types root (covered by `wiresmith-7m6` BUF-2.2).

**Validation:**
- Add `proto/basic/multi_root_a/foo.proto` + `proto/basic/multi_root_b/bar.proto` where `bar.proto` imports `foo.proto`.
- CLI test invoking with `--proto_path=proto/basic/multi_root_a --proto_path=proto/basic/multi_root_b` and asserting both files generate.
- Negative test: same import path present in both roots → expect explicit collision error.

title: GRPC-4: Support streaming RPCs (client-stream, server-stream, bidi)
description:
**Parent:** wiresmith-36b (gRPC umbrella).
**Depends on:** GRPC-2 (client), GRPC-3 (server).

**What:** Extend client and server emission to handle all three streaming modes. Without this, the umbrella is unusable for Mimir's push API and any other streaming consumer.

**Client side, per streaming method:**
```go
// server-stream
type Foo_ListServer = grpc.ServerStreamingClient[ListResponse]
func (c *fooClient) List(ctx context.Context, in *ListRequest, opts ...grpc.CallOption) (Foo_ListClient, error)

// client-stream / bidi: similar with grpc.ClientStreamingClient / grpc.BidiStreamingClient
```
Use the typed `grpc.ClientStream` helpers (Go 1.21+ generic gRPC, available in `google.golang.org/grpc` ≥ 1.65).

**Server side, per streaming method:**
- Emit a stream handler thunk that wraps the `grpc.ServerStream` in the typed iterator.
- Append `grpc.StreamDesc{StreamName, Handler, ServerStreams, ClientStreams}` entries to the service's `Streams` slice (populated empty in GRPC-3).

**Key requirements:**
- All three streaming kinds in one bead — they share infrastructure.
- Method path format unchanged (`/pkg.Svc/Method`).
- ServiceDesc.Streams populated correctly: server-stream → `ServerStreams: true`, client-stream → `ClientStreams: true`, bidi → both true.

**Validation:** Per-stream-kind integration tests, all three in `test/peer/grpc_streaming_test.go`. Drive a 10-message round-trip per direction.

title: GRPC-3: Generate gRPC server interface + ServiceDesc + handler thunks
description:
**Parent:** wiresmith-36b (gRPC umbrella).
**Depends on:** GRPC-1 (descriptor parsing).

**What:** Emit, per service:
```go
type FooServer interface {
    Bar(context.Context, *BarRequest) (*BarResponse, error)
}
func RegisterFooServer(s grpc.ServiceRegistrar, srv FooServer) {
    s.RegisterService(&Foo_ServiceDesc, srv)
}
var Foo_ServiceDesc = grpc.ServiceDesc{
    ServiceName: "pkg.Foo",
    HandlerType: (*FooServer)(nil),
    Methods: []grpc.MethodDesc{{ MethodName: "Bar", Handler: _Foo_Bar_Handler }},
    Streams: []grpc.StreamDesc{},  // populated in GRPC-4
    Metadata: "...source filename...",
}
func _Foo_Bar_Handler(srv interface{}, ctx context.Context, dec func(interface{}) error, interceptor grpc.UnaryServerInterceptor) (interface{}, error) { /* dec into *BarRequest, call srv.Bar, return */ }
```

Match `protoc-gen-go-grpc`'s shape. The handler thunk is the bridge between gRPC's generic dispatch and the typed server interface.

**Key requirements:**
- Unary methods only in this bead (streaming in GRPC-4).
- ServiceDesc.Metadata = path of the source .proto file (gRPC reflection consumes this).
- Emit `RegisterFooServer` as the public registration API.
- Optional: emit `UnimplementedFooServer` with default-method stubs that return `codes.Unimplemented` (matches `protoc-gen-go-grpc` since v1.32 — useful for forward compatibility).

**Validation:** Integration test in `test/peer/grpc_server_test.go`: register a wiresmith-generated server with `grpc.NewServer`, call via the generated client from GRPC-2, assert round-trip.

title: GRPC-2: Generate gRPC client interface + implementation
GRPC-1: Parse service / rpc definitions from descriptors
description:
**Parent:** wiresmith-36b (gRPC umbrella).

**What:** Today the generator ignores `service` declarations in `.proto` files. Extend `compiler/generator/` to read `protoreflect.FileDescriptor.Services()` and surface the data needed by GRPC-2/3/4: service name, fully-qualified method paths, input/output types, streaming kind (unary, client-stream, server-stream, bidi).

**Plumbing only — no code emission in this bead.** Produce an internal model (`ServiceModel`, `MethodModel`) the emit phases consume. Wire it into `generateFile` phase ordering (likely after enums/messages so method input/output type names are already resolved).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- A `proto/basic/service.proto` fixture with one unary, one client-stream, one server-stream, one bidi RPC.
- Unit test asserts the generator parses all four with correct streaming kinds and input/output type names.
- No `.pb.go` change yet — emission is deferred to GRPC-2/3.

**Out of scope:** code emission (GRPC-2/3), streaming runtime behavior (GRPC-4).

title: JSON-3: Oneof JSON encoding/decoding (variant tag handling)
description:
**Parent:** wiresmith-4kx (JSON/text umbrella).

**What:** Extend JSON-1 and JSON-2 to handle `oneof` fields per proto3 JSON spec: serialize only the active variant using its proto field name as the JSON key, deserialize by detecting which variant key is present.

**Why this needs its own bead:** Oneofs are the single trickiest part of proto JSON. wiresmith's oneof representation (interface + per-variant wrapper struct) maps cleanly to JSON but requires special-case emit in both marshal and unmarshal. TEMPO.md:69-75 calls out `AnyValue` (oneof-based) as a primary motivator — `encoding/json` cannot handle wiresmith oneofs natively, which is why Tempo hand-rolls a custom marshaler today.

**Key requirements:**
- Marshal: type-switch on the oneof interface, emit the variant's JSON key + payload.
- Unmarshal: tentatively parse to map[string]json.RawMessage, detect which oneof key is present, dispatch to the variant's decoder. Reject if multiple variants of the same oneof are set.
- `AnyValue` round-trip via wiresmith JSON → Tempo, side-by-side with the hand-rolled implementation, until parity is verified.

**Validation:** Reuse `proto/basic/oneof.proto` fixtures. Add a `test/basic/json_oneof_test.go`. Cross-check against `protojson` (treat its output as canonical).

title: JSON-2: Generate UnmarshalJSON per message
description:
**Parent:** wiresmith-4kx (JSON/text umbrella).

**What:** Emit `UnmarshalJSON([]byte) error` per generated message. Inverse of JSON-1.

**Key requirements:**
- Accept both `lowerCamelCase` (proto3 JSON canonical) and the original proto field name (`protojson` default).
- int64/uint64/fixed64/sfixed64/sint64 accept **JSON strings OR numbers** (spec mandates either is valid input even though we emit strings).
- bytes accept base64 (standard padded; URL-safe is a follow-up).
- Enums accept name (string) or number (int). Unknown enum names → error; unknown numbers → preserve as int.
- `null` for singular fields → leave default (or nil for `optional`).
- Map JSON objects → map values.
- Maintain wiresmith's no-unknown-field-preservation contract: unknown JSON keys are silently ignored (matches `protojson.UnmarshalOptions{DiscardUnknown: true}`).

**Out of scope:** oneofs (see JSON-3), text format (see JSON-4).

**Validation:** Round-trip `MarshalJSON → UnmarshalJSON → Equal` fuzz target in `test/fuzz/`. Differential against `protojson.Unmarshal` for the OTel macros.

title: JSON-1: Generate MarshalJSON per message (proto3 JSON spec)
description:
**Parent:** wiresmith-4kx (JSON/text umbrella).

**What:** Emit a `MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error)` method per generated message that produces proto3 JSON spec-compliant output. Handles scalars, nested messages, repeated, maps, and presence (omit unset singular non-optional fields per spec).

**Key requirements:**
- Field names: `lowerCamelCase` JSON keys derived from proto names (matches official `protojson`).
- int64/uint64/fixed64/sfixed64/sint64 encode as **JSON strings** per OTLP JSON spec (TEMPO.md:52 calls this out specifically).
- bytes → base64.
- `optional` fields omit when nil; non-optional singular omit when at default (zero).
- Maps → JSON objects (key stringified per scalar key type).
- Enums emit by name (string), with int fallback for unknown values matching the official runtime's `EmitUnpopulated`/`UseEnumNumbers` knobs (start with name-emit; add knobs later if needed).

**Out of scope:** oneofs (see JSON-3), text format (see JSON-4), WKT JSON encoding (covered by BUF-2.2 `wiresmith-7m6` whenever WKT lands).

**Validation:** Differential test against `google.golang.org/protobuf/encoding/protojson` for the OTel macros (Traces, Metrics, Logs) and `proto/basic/*`. Expect bit-identical except where field iteration order is undefined.

title: Add gRPC service stub generation (umbrella)
description:
**Source:** MIMIR.md "gRPC service stubs" (lines 36-37). BUF.md is silent on services.

**Why:** `AGENTS.md:91` and `docs/design.md:39` both list "services/RPCs" as Not Supported because OTel collector hot paths only need message serialization. Mimir, Tempo, and other Grafana consumers also need wiresmith to generate gRPC server/client code so it can fully replace gogofaster/protoc-gen-go-grpc. Without service stubs, wiresmith remains an unmarshal-only library for consumers with internal gRPC APIs.

**Approach:** Parse `service`/`rpc` definitions via `bufbuild/protocompile` (already used for messages). Emit:
- Client interface + Client impl (`grpc.ClientConnInterface`-backed)
- Server interface + ServiceDesc + handler thunks
- Streaming RPC support (client-stream, server-stream, bidi)

Match `protoc-gen-go-grpc` shape so consumers can swap in wiresmith without touching call sites. Runtime dependency: `google.golang.org/grpc` (standard).

**Design decisions to revisit when this lands:**
- `docs/design.md:39` "No proto2, services/RPCs, extensions..." — services clause must be revised.
- `AGENTS.md:91` "Not supported (not needed for OTel protos): services/RPCs..." — services clause must be revised.

**Decomposition:** see linked children — descriptor parsing, client gen, server gen, streaming. Update docs as part of umbrella close.

**Out of scope:** non-gRPC service exports (Twirp, Connect-Go, etc.). Reflection-service registration (since field-level reflection still panics).

title: Add protojson / prototext serialization support (umbrella)
description:
**Source:** TEMPO.md "AnyValue JSON handling" + MIMIR.md "Well-known type helpers" + IMPROVEMENTS.md gap.

**Why:** `docs/design.md` "Limitations" currently states `protojson`/`prototext` are unsupported because they depend on field-level reflection (Range/Get/Set/Mutable), which panics on wiresmith types — value-type message fields are incompatible with `protoimpl`'s field converters. Tempo and Mimir both need JSON serialization for OTLP attribute values (`AnyValue`), jsonpb interop, and OTLP JSON spec compliance (int64/uint64 encoded as JSON strings). The current consumer workaround in TEMPO.md:69-88 is hand-rolled `UnmarshalJSON`/`MarshalJSON` per-type, which does not scale.

**Approach:** Skip the protoreflect.Message route entirely — generate type-specific `MarshalJSON`/`UnmarshalJSON` (and `MarshalText`/`UnmarshalText`) directly, gogo-style. Matches `gogoproto.jsonpb` integration shape but emitted unconditionally per generated message. Avoids the field-level reflection gap.

**Design decisions to revisit when this lands:**
- `docs/design.md:38` "No `protojson`/`prototext`" — must be revised.
- `AGENTS.md:91` "well-known types" caveat is orthogonal; WKT JSON encoding is tracked separately under BUF-2.2 (`wiresmith-7m6`).

**Decomposition:** see linked children — Marshal, Unmarshal, oneof handling, text-proto format. Update docs as part of umbrella close.

**Out of scope:** field-level reflection (`Range`/`Get`/`Set`/`Mutable`) — those panics stay; this umbrella sidesteps them entirely.

title: map<K, Msg> duplicate-key wire entries should REPLACE not MERGE
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `compiler/types/map.go::EmitUnmarshal` (post-loop merge branch, ~line 123).

**What:** When the same map key appears more than once on the wire, wiresmith MERGES the two message values via `existing.unmarshal(mapValueBytes, depth+1)` (or `existing.UnmarshalWithDepth` cross-package). The proto3 spec and the reference `google.golang.org/protobuf` runtime use REPLACE semantics — the second wire entry's value REPLACES the first entirely. See protobuf-go's `internal/impl/codec_map.go::consumeMapOfMessage`: each entry allocates a fresh `val`, unmarshals into it, and then `SetMapIndex` overwrites whatever was there.

**Discovered via:** wiresmith-sb1 re-enabled `corecursive`/`recursive_message` in the conformance proto. Two of the three previously expected recursive failures pass cleanly. The third — `Required.Proto3.ProtobufInput.ValidDataMap.STRING.MESSAGE.MergeValue.ProtobufOutput` — now exposes the merge-vs-replace divergence. Conformance output shows our merged corecursive carries fields from BOTH wire entries; the reference carries only the second.

**Fix:** Drop the merge branch in MapField.EmitUnmarshal for message-valued maps. Allocate a fresh value, decode the entry's bytes into it, then unconditionally `m.X[mapkey] = decoded_value`. Three observations to preserve:
  1. Same-package vs cross-package decode (the depth-threading from wiresmith-1c0) is still required for the value's per-field decode — only the outer merge call goes away.
  2. The wiresmith-1c0 generator tests pin the merge call's shape; they need to be replaced with tests that assert `SetMapIndex`-style replace.
  3. The CLAUDE.md 'Map field correctness → Merge semantics' note about `mapValueBytes != nil` vs `len(mapValueBytes) > 0` is about WITHIN a single map entry (the value field being absent vs empty-but-present) — that part stays correct.

**Validate:** After the fix, `Required.Proto3.ProtobufInput.ValidDataMap.STRING.MESSAGE.MergeValue.ProtobufOutput` should pass and can be removed from `test/conformance/failure_list.txt`. Conformance should go to 700 passing, 2 expected failures.

title: SEC-5 regression: cross-package map merge calls public Unmarshal (depth-reset)
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `compiler/types/map.go:127`.

**What:** When a map\<K, Msg\> field has a value message in a different package, duplicate-key merge emits `existing.Unmarshal(mapValueBytes)` — the public entry point that resets the unmarshal recursion-depth counter to 0. This re-opens the SEC-5 hole that `UnmarshalWithDepth` closes everywhere else for cross-package message access:

- Same-package map merge: `existing.unmarshal(mapValueBytes, depth+1)` ✓
- Cross-package map merge: `existing.Unmarshal(mapValueBytes)` ✗ (depth-reset)
- Cross-package initial-value-decode (`MessageType.EmitMapEntryUnmarshal`, message.go:88): `varName.UnmarshalWithDepth(dAtA[iNdEx:postIndex], depth+1)` ✓ — the fix that 4a547a4 / SEC-5 was specifically about, but the merge path was not updated.

**Discovered via:** TEST-2 (wiresmith-thc) coverage. `TestMapField_EmitUnmarshal_CrossPackageMessageMerge` deliberately does NOT pin the exact public-call symbol (`Unmarshal` vs `UnmarshalWithDepth`) — that decision was deferred to this bead so the unit test doesn't guard the bug. It does assert the forward-compatible invariants (nil-based merge predicate, private `existing.unmarshal()` not reachable cross-package).

**Fix:** Emit `existing.UnmarshalWithDepth(mapValueBytes, depth+1)` for the cross-package merge branch. Add a generator-level integration test analogous to TestGenerateMapEntryThreadsDepth (generator_test.go:~1062) targeting the duplicate-key merge wire-form, not the initial decode.

**Validate:** add a regression test that crafts a wire input with N+1 duplicate map keys, each carrying a deeply-recursive payload, and asserts that the total decoded depth equals the cumulative depth (i.e. depth tracking survives the merge boundary).
notes:
Fix: changed compiler/types/map.go:127 from existing.Unmarshal(mapValueBytes) to existing.UnmarshalWithDepth(mapValueBytes, depth+1) so cross-package duplicate-key merge threads the recursion-depth counter, matching the rest of the codegen.

Tests added/updated:
- compiler/generator/generator_test.go: TestGenerateCrossPackageMapDuplicateKeyMergeThreadsDepth (new). Pins the codegen string match exactly the way TestGenerateCrossPackageMapValueThreadsDepth pins the *initial* decode site — the merge branch is now under string-match coverage too. Verified the test fails on pre-fix code.
- compiler/types/map_test.go: TestMapField_EmitUnmarshal_CrossPackageMessageMerge updated to drop the previously deliberate 'don't pin the call symbol' carve-out and assert UnmarshalWithDepth(...,depth+1) explicitly.

New proto: proto/basic/crossmap.proto defines basic.crossmap.v1.CrossMapOuter with a map<string, basic.recursive.v1.LinkedList> field, giving us a generator-test fixture without needing t.TempDir scaffolding.

Runtime test the bead recommended was investigated and dropped. The setup the bead suggests (N+1 duplicate keys with deeply-recursive payloads) cannot differentiate pre/post-fix at runtime: MessageType.EmitMapEntryUnmarshal already routes the *initial* per-entry value decode through depth+1, so any payload that would trip the post-fix merge also trips the initial decode of that same entry, before the merge runs. The pre-fix merge starts at depth=0 (strictly under the budget), so the only way to expose the exploit at the wire level is via a cross-package CYCLE (Outer -> map<K, Leaf>; Leaf -> Outer) so depth accumulates across the package boundary before reaching the merge — that's the SEC-5 attack model. The current proto suite doesn't have such a cycle and adding one purely for this test would be scope creep. The generator-level + unit-level string-match coverage is the practical guard; matches how the original SEC-5 fix is locked in (no runtime test there either).
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/94
close_reason: implemented: cross-package map merge now threads depth via existing.UnmarshalWithDepth(mapValueBytes, depth+1)

title: Use --out to compose the Go import-path base (drop /gen/ hardcode)
description:
**Source:** Highlighted by review on PR #79 (DOC-2). The README now invites users to set --module to their own module path, but the import-path base in compiler/generator/names.go:destForPath is hardcoded as `joinImport(module, "gen")`. Cross-file imports emitted in generated code therefore always reference `<module>/gen/<source-rel>`, regardless of --out. A user picking --out=pkg/api gets files on disk at pkg/api/foo/v1/foo.pb.go with imports pointing at <module>/gen/foo/v1 — Go's directory-equals-import-path rule rejects this at build time.
